articles.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
articles.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

articles.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
articles.description: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.

articles.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
articles.description: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

articles.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
articles.description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

articles.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
articles.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

articles.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
articles.description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

articles.title: Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access
articles.description: Several users on social media reported having their Instagram accounts hacked over the weekend. Meta's own support chatbot was blamed for allowing hackers to hijack accounts.

articles.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
articles.description: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

articles.title: A new app, The Mall, is building a universal feed for online shopping
articles.description: The Mall lets shoppers build a personalized feed of brands, track sales and drops, and discover products across thousands of retailers.

articles.title: Grand Theft Auto V cheat service gets hacked, exposing thousands of gamers
articles.description: Hackers stole usernames, hashed passwords, and other data from a service that allowed players to cheat in Grand Theft Auto V.

articles.title: Zigging when most are zagging, ex-Meta CTO raises $250M climate fund
articles.description: Mike Schroepfer's Gigascale Capital has raised a large fund to back founders building climate-friendly solutions for the world's energy and material shortages.

articles.title: Anthropic files to go public
articles.description: Anthropic, now an AI powerhouse that has landed top-tier enterprise customers, was once considered an underdog in the emerging world of large language models.

articles.title: SpaceX says it may issue ‘significant’ equity in ‘future transactions’
articles.description: The company added a warning to prospective investors that a major dilution could be in the cards after it goes public.

articles.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies
articles.description: WindBorne benefits from its unique combination of model-building and data collection. The company now has about 400 balloons in flight gathering sensor readings at any given time, launched from 15 sites around the globe. The advances in its current model come from improvements in how the data collected by the balloons is fed into the models.

articles.title: DuckDuckGo makes its ‘no-AI’ search engine easier to access as its traffic booms
articles.description: Alternative search engine DuckDuckGo launches 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox users.

articles.title: Revolut rolls out services to thousands of users in India ahead of broader launch
articles.description: The British fintech has built a waitlist of about 450,000 users in India as it prepares for a broader launch.

articles.title: Strava declares war on scrapers ahead of IPO
articles.description: To stop scraping, the company is increasing security around its website and will now only allow authenticated users to view certain data. Earlier, users were able to see details like public profiles and fitness club listings without logging in. The company is putting all that data behind authentication to protect it from unauthorized AI scraping.

articles.title: Unastella, a South Korean rocket startup that launched from home, raises $24M
articles.description: The Seoul-based rocket startup is developing its own launch vehicles and engines.

articles.title: Erin Brockovich takes aim at data center secrecy
articles.description: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

articles.title: This weekend’s two biggest movies were both directed by YouTubers
articles.description: The YouTube-to-prestige-horror pipeline is looking very strong.

articles.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
articles.description: Some report burning through their whole monthly "AI credit" allotment in a single day.

articles.title: Why cats prefer silver vine to catnip and other May highlights
articles.description: Prehistoric mining in the Pyrenees, a new species of tiny blue octopus, slapstick acoustics, and more.

articles.title: Moderna gets $50 million to develop mRNA Ebola vaccine against Bundibugyo
articles.description: Amid a raging Ebola outbreak, officials "urgently accelerate development" of vaccines.

articles.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
articles.description: Pricey Instagram handles were stolen and resold before Meta patched the exploit.

articles.title: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra looks like its first true MacBook Pro competitor
articles.description: It's Microsoft's least-weird attempt at a high-end mobile workstation.

articles.title: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel
articles.description: Anyone who has downloaded affected Red Hat packages should investigate immediately.

articles.title: New Trump vaccine order based on "no credible scientific evidence," doctors say
articles.description: Even Danish researchers think it's bizarre.

articles.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman after multiple ChatGPT-linked murders
articles.description: Altman has an "utter disregard" for human lives, Florida AG says.

articles.title: From 15 hours to one minute: How AI/ML is speeding up GM's development
articles.description: From CFD and FEA to digital twins, carmaking now involves a lot of virtualization.

articles.title: Allegedly trashing Airbnbs to test robots puts startup in legal trouble
articles.description: Lawsuit seeks $12,000 from startup that allegedly damaged home in robot tests.

articles.title: AMD extends Socket AM5 support through at least 2029; AM4 refuses to die
articles.description: The 5800X3D returns at $349, while the 7700X3D debuts at $329.

articles.title: ROG Xbox Ally X20 adds OLED screen, control upgrades
articles.description: But the hardware refresh is tethered to a bundle with pricey AR glasses.

articles.title: Nvidia RTX Spark comes to Windows PCs with Arm CPU, RTX GPU, and unified memory
articles.description: Nvidia's new chips will power laptop workstations and mini desktop PCs at first.

articles.title: Intel: Our upcoming AI chip will be cheaper, run cooler than Nvidia, AMD options
articles.description: Crescent Island is an air-cooled chip that uses LPDDR5 memory.

articles.title: An OpenAI model solved a famous math problem that stumped humans for 80 years
articles.description: I tried to explain OpenAI’s solution more clearly than OpenAI did.

articles.title: On its 40th anniversary, we reassess 1986's SpaceCamp
articles.description: Is it a hidden gem, a cult classic, or hopelessly dumb? We vote "all of the above."

articles.title: They call it stupid hot for a reason: Heat muddles animal brains
articles.description: As temperatures rise, some creatures pick fights while others struggle to learn.

articles.title: Grifters, cynics, and true believers: The family tree of vaccine opponents
articles.description: A new book looks into the long history of people who have opposed vaccines.

articles.title: Environmentalists turn out in force to oppose Trump coal ash rollbacks
articles.description: Trump admin wants to rely on states for coal ash monitoring, enforcement, allow them to bypass national standards.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI领域大额投资与IPO动态**：Alphabet计划筹集800亿美元用于AI基础设施建设；Anthropic提交上市申请；前Meta CTO逆势筹集2.5亿美元气候基金。
2. **芯片与硬件竞争**：英伟达瞄准2000亿美元CPU市场，联合微软、戴尔、惠普推出AI智能体PC。
3. **国防与航天科技估值飙升**：国防科技公司Mach Industries估值一年内暴涨4倍至18亿美元；SpaceX将水资源获取列为IPO风险因素，并暗示未来可能大规模发行股权。
4. **网络安全与法律纠纷**：黑客利用Meta AI客服漏洞劫持Instagram账号；佛罗里达州首次起诉OpenAI及Sam Altman，指控其与暴力事件相关；《GTA V》作弊服务遭黑客攻击，数万玩家数据泄露。
5. **新兴应用与搜索引擎趋势**：新应用“The Mall”打造统一在线购物信息流；DuckDuckGo在流量激增背景下强化其“无AI”搜索引擎的易用性。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI领域资本狂潮持续升温，从Alphabet的800亿美元基建投资到Anthropic的IPO，同时国防与航天科技估值飙升，但网络安全漏洞与法律诉讼也为行业敲响警钟。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型与框架更新**：JetBrains发布12B参数的混合专家模型Mellum2；NVIDIA推出首个开源全模态模型Cosmos 3，用于物理AI推理与行动；PyTorch发布torch.profiler入门指南，助力性能分析。
2. **企业级AI应用与逻辑**：文章强调可扩展的企业AI采用依赖于智能体逻辑（Agent Logic），而非仅依赖大语言模型；TRL通过Hub Bucket实现万亿参数模型的增量权重同步，提升部署效率。
3. **Google I/O 2026前瞻**：Google展示了Gemini在I/O 2026中的实际应用（如构建活动网站、生成互动测验），并推出Gemini Omni和Gemini 3.5的9个演示demo，以及未来实验室的AI原型。
4. **OpenAI与行业合作**：OpenAI在密歇根州建设智能时代基础设施；前沿模型及Codex已上线AWS；波士顿儿童医院利用AI发现新诊断方法；Braintrust借助Codex将客户需求转化为代码。
5. **机器人本地化与开源**：Reachy Mini机器人实现完全本地化运行，无需云端依赖。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Google I/O 2026密集展示了Gemini系列模型在真实场景中的落地能力，结合NVIDIA开源物理AI模型和OpenAI与AWS的深度合作，表明2026年AI正从“模型竞赛”转向“全栈工程化与行业渗透”阶段。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中美科技对抗升级**：美国拟禁止中国高科技汽车进口，并限制向中国境外中企出口AI芯片，同时欧盟与中国贸易摩擦加剧，双方濒临贸易战边缘。
2. **中国航天与脑机接口突破**：中国首次成功发射可重复使用火箭（对标SpaceX Falcon 9），并批准侵入式脑机接口芯片临床试验，展现脑植入技术雄心。
3. **AI应用与风险管控**：中国将AI用于预测政治风险，同时美国指控中国“AI窃取”，中方在药物研发领域崛起对美国构成压力。
4. **全球产业布局与市场拓展**：欧盟担忧中国在摩洛哥建设工业基地，中国车企进入美国市场（如得州埃尔帕索），并针对中国穆斯林群体开展销售。
5. **特斯拉在华诉讼**：特斯拉“全自动驾驶”欺诈案在中国首次开庭，涉及消费者权益与自动驾驶技术争议。

今日最值得关注的发展：中美在芯片、AI及汽车领域的对抗持续升级，同时中国在航天、脑机接口等前沿技术领域取得突破性进展。
